qa_pairs.question: What types of resources can research groups access through the Rapid Access Service (RAS)?
qa_pairs.answer: Through the Rapid Access Service (RAS), research groups can access modest quantities of storage and cloud resources after they register for an account. Opportunistic access to compute resources is also possible.

qa_pairs.question: Is priority given for compute resources accessed through the Rapid Access Service (RAS)?
qa_pairs.answer: No, priority is only given to those with an allocation for compute resources accessed through the Rapid Access Service (RAS).

qa_pairs.question: Can research groups satisfy all their needs using only the Rapid Access Service (RAS)?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, many research groups can meet their needs through RAS only.

qa_pairs.question: Can a researcher submit more than one application as the lead applicant for the Resources for Research Groups (RRG) process?
qa_pairs.answer: No, a Principal Investigator (PI) can only be the lead applicant for one RRG application.

qa_pairs.question: Can Principal Investigators (PIs) participate as collaborators on multiple RRG proposals?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Principal Investigators (PIs) can participate as collaborators on other projects submitting RRG proposals.
